methodology.tau2_bench_version: tau2-bench main checkout used for local evaluation; see checklist for local path and result hashes
methodology.notes: Custom text submission. Agent is the DRQ-27B TauSynth-GRIPO-Multistage model, based on a Qwen3.5-27B model trained through three stages: (1) GRIPO RL on tau-style synthetic trajectories and tau-bench-style interaction data, (2) GRIPO-MOPD second-stage trajectory/tool-use repair and multi-checkpoint distillation to mitigate seesaw regressions, and (3) GRIPO-SFT OpenAI-format instruction-following training for chat/tool-call protocol compliance. The resulting merged model was served through a local vLLM OpenAI-compatible endpoint as model tau27_targeted_sft_merged. User simulator was gpt-5.5 for all four domains. The three core tau3 text domains (airline, retail, telecom) were evaluated on the base split with 4 trials per task and no task filters. Reported Pass^k uses C(c,k)/C(n,k) per task, then arithmetic mean over tasks; aggregate core-domain averages, if reported separately, use the unweighted mean over airline, retail, and telecom. Training methodology: GRIPO RL: Agentic reinforcement learning based on tau-style synthetic trajectories and tau-bench-style interaction data, producing the step500 GRIPO posttraining checkpoint used as the main initialization. GRIPO-MOPD two-stage training: A second-stage trajectory and tool-use repair phase that applies MOPD-style multi-teacher or multi-checkpoint distillation to reduce seesaw regressions across tau3 domains and consolidate complementary checkpoint behaviors. GRIPO-SFT OpenAI-format instruction-following training: Supervised fine-tuning on OpenAI-compatible chat and tool-call format data to improve instruction following, function-call formatting, schema-valid arguments, and local vLLM OpenAI-compatible serving behavior. Secondary contact: ryantianyuli@aliyun.com.

verification.details: Full core-domain runs: airline 50x4, retail 114x4, telecom 114x4. No infrastructure_error terminations in the final core-domain results. Raw trajectory/results files are hosted externally in the trajectory repository.
